title: Compose only through public LEDGER contracts
description: Consume version-compatible public LEDGER category and Budget Actual operations without private storage access or financial-semantic duplication. Implements G4 and G5 through UC-BUDGET-001, UC-BUDGET-002, and UC-BUDGET-003.
rationale: BUDGET is trustworthy only if canonical category and actual semantics remain owned by LEDGER.
story_want: budget planning to use canonical Ledger categories and actuals
story_so_that: plan comparisons reconcile with the financial source of truth
acceptance_criteria: Given BUDGET starts an operation requiring LEDGER, when compatibility is checked, then the required category, Budget Actual, snapshot, error, and contract versions are verified before plan mutation or position calculation.

Given category validation is required, when BUDGET reads category state, then it uses the public LEDGER category identity and lifecycle result without maintaining an authoritative duplicate catalogue.

Given a Budget Position request, when BUDGET obtains actuals, then it requests Budget Actual values for the exact Budget Period and current category and categorization state under one cited LEDGER snapshot.

Given LEDGER returns transfer, refund, correction, allocation, void, supersession, or lifecycle-aware Budget Actual values, when BUDGET consumes them, then those values are used verbatim and no financial transaction is reclassified or reinterpreted.
Given LEDGER actuals require multiple pages, when BUDGET completes the query, then every page belongs to the same snapshot and every returned member is accounted for exactly once.

Given the LEDGER snapshot expires or any page, integrity check, or contract call fails, when position calculation ends, then BUDGET returns a stable unavailable result and emits no partial position or totals.
Given any BUDGET workflow, when public LEDGER operations are inspected, then zero account, transaction, relationship, category-assignment, or lifecycle mutation is requested.

Given an unsupported or incompatible LEDGER contract version, when a dependent BUDGET operation is requested, then it fails before plan mutation or financial-data result and does not fall back to private storage.
security_criteria: BUDGET may use only public LEDGER operation contracts and never access private LEDGER storage, handlers, or implementation types.
